prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [GOLD] Source: Intermittent fasting improves metabolic outcomes in metaboli | Statement: Ten RCTs with 701 individuals were pooled to evaluate IF effects on glycemic control, lipid profile, and inflammatory markers.
2. [GOLD] Source: Intermittent fasting improves metabolic outcomes in metaboli | Statement: IF reduced fasting blood sugar with pooled SMD = -0.51 (95% CI: -0.81, -0.20; p = 0.001).
3. [GOLD] Source: Intermittent fasting improves metabolic outcomes in metaboli | Statement: IF reduced fasting insulin with pooled SMD = -0.27 (95% CI: -0.52, -0.03; p = 0.027).
4. [SILVER] Source: Intermittent Fasting and Time-Restricted Eating Role in Diet | Statement: IF, time-restricted eating, and fasting-mimicking diets have emerged as weight loss programs contributing to improved cardiometabolic health.
5. [SILVER] Source: Effect of time-restricted eating on neurovascular coupling a | Statement: Participants were instructed to maintain a 10:14 intermittent fasting regimen daily for 6 months.
